methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Infrastructure & Logistics (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Infrastructure & Logistics, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Amperon is a Series B infrastructure and logistics company that has raised $20M to date. The comparable universe in this sector is sparse: one private peer (AiDash) was identified, while no public comparables exist. The narrowness of the peer set—particularly the absence of same-stage or valuation-tier matches—precludes meaningful median revenue multiple or precedent transaction analysis.

Stage-progression methodology is the sole applicable triangulation approach. Using total capital raised ($20M) as a valuation proxy and applying a standard Series B-to-subsequent-stage step-up multiplier of 1.9x yields an implied valuation of $38M. This represents the central estimate given the methodological constraints.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $32M (low) to $44M (high), with a median of $38M. The single-method derivation and data scarcity around Infrastructure & Logistics peers constitute material uncertainties. Validation would benefit from revenue or ARR data, customer concentration metrics, and expansion of the peer universe beyond the current one identified private company. Any upward or downward revision from the $38M median should be anchored to company-specific unit economics and market traction rather than sector multiples, which remain undefined here.
